input.description: Explore codebase structure for metrics implementation
input.prompt:
Explore the DDx codebase to understand:

1. The structure of `cli/internal/agent/` - especially `execute_bead_loop.go` and any related files
2. The events.jsonl format - what fields are in the events written to `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl`
3. The result.json and manifest.json formats used in `.ddx/executions/`
4. How the `ddx work` command is structured in `cli/cmd/`
5. Any existing metrics-related code

Search breadth: very thorough. Read the actual file contents for:
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` (if exists)
- Any `*_loop*.go` files in the agent package
- `cli/cmd/work*.go` files
- The events.jsonl schema (find any Go structs that write events)
- `cli/internal/bead/` package structure

Report the full contents of the key files and the overall directory structure.

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
68		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
69		Mode         executeloop.Mode
70		IdleInterval time.Duration
71		Once         bool
72		PollInterval time.Duration
73		NoReview     bool
74		LabelFilter  string
75		SessionID    string
76		WorkerID     string
77		ProjectRoot  string
78		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
79		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
80		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
81		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
82		TargetBeadID string
83		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
84		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
85		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
86		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
87		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
88		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
89		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
90	}
91	
92	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
93		mode := r.Mode
94		if mode == "" {
95			if r.Once {
96				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
97			} else {
98				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
99			}
100		}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
91		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
92		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
93		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
94			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
95		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
96		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
97		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99	
100		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", and "ddx work clear-cooldowns" as subcommands.
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
102		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
103		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
104	
105		return cmd
106	}
107	
108	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
109	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
110	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
111	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
112	// by this layer.
113	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
114		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
115			return err
116		}
117	
118		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
119		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
120	
121		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
122		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
123		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
124		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
125		if !asJSON {
126			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
127			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
128				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
129			})
130		}
131	
132		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
133	}

9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.

12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`

15		Status    string    `json:"status"`

17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`

20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}

76	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
77	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
78	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
79	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
80	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
81	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
82	// will fail CI.
83	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{

86		StatusClosed,

88		StatusProposed,

92	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
93	// in CanonicalStatuses.
94	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
95		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
96			if c == s {
97				return true
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}

106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)
109	
110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)

121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}

190		MaxPriority     = 4

241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}

250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}

260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
11	type Config struct {
12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
21	}

30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
33	}

35	// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
36	type RunArgs struct {
37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context
42		Harness       string
43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
45		PromptSource  string
46		Correlation   map[string]string
47		Model         string
48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
49		ModelRef      string // opaque model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved by Fizeau
50		Effort        string
51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
53		WorkDir       string
54		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
55		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
56		Role          string
57		CorrelationID string
58		Env           map[string]string
59	}

61	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
62	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
63	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
64	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
65	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
66	// runtime intent remain.
67	//
68	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
69	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
70		Prompt                string
71		PromptFile            string
72		PromptSource          string
73		Output                io.Writer // optional live service-event progress sink
74		Correlation           map[string]string
75		Record                bool
76		Replay                bool
77		WorkDir               string
78		EstimatedPromptTokens int
79		RequiresTools         bool
80		SessionLogDirOverride string
81		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, ModelRefOverride, ProfileOverride, and
82		// PermissionsOverride let a caller override one durable knob from rcfg for
83		// this single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
84		// SD-024 step B22d-d: execute-bead sets permissions=unrestricted; hidden
85		// lifecycle calls use profile-only routing hints sourced from fizeau.
86		HarnessOverride     string
87		ModelOverride       string
88		ModelRefOverride    string
89		ProfileOverride     string
90		PermissionsOverride string
91		// ClearRoutingPins drops rcfg Harness/Provider/Model/ModelRef passthrough for
92		// hidden DDx lifecycle calls. Explicit runtime overrides still win.
93		ClearRoutingPins bool
94		Role             string
95		CorrelationID    string
96		Env              map[string]string
97		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
98		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
99		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
100		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).
101		MinPowerOverride int
102		// ClearMinPower drops rcfg.MinPower() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
103		// lifecycle classifier calls should not inherit implementation power bounds;
104		// Fizeau owns routing for those hidden dispatches.
105		ClearMinPower bool
106		// ClearMaxPower drops rcfg.MaxPower() for this single invocation. This is
107		// for auxiliary classifier/reviewer-style calls that deliberately want the
108		// strongest viable route instead of the worker attempt's cost cap.
109		ClearMaxPower bool
110		// ClearProfile drops rcfg.Profile() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
111		// calls select their own fizeau profile hints instead of inheriting the
112		// worker attempt's profile.
113		ClearProfile bool
114	}

401		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
402		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
403		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
404		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
405		// recommendation.
406		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
407		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
408		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
409		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
410		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
411		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
412		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
413		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
414		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
415		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
416		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
417		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
418		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
419		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
420		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
421		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
422		// occurring.
423		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
424		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
425		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
426		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
427		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
428		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
429		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
430		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
431	}
432	
433	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
434		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)

1	// Package agentmetrics loads attempt outcomes for Story 11 aggregations
2	// (success rate, cost, duration per harness/provider/model/tier).
3	//
4	// Source priority is FEAT-010 run-store first (.ddx/exec/runs/*.json) with
5	// .ddx/executions/*/result.json as the legacy fallback. Per-attempt records
6	// are deduped by AttemptID — run-store wins over bundles when both exist —
7	// matching the multi-source dedupe used by state_runs.go (ddx-65543530).
8	//
9	// Per the Story 11 locked decision, the already_satisfied terminal status
10	// counts as success. See Bucket.Successful.
11	package agentmetrics
12	
13	import "time"
14	
15	// Source identifies where an Attempt was loaded from.
16	const (
17		SourceRunStore = "run-store"
18		SourceBundle   = "bundle"
19	)
20	
21	// Attempt is one execute-bead try projected to the fields Story 11
22	// aggregations need. Cost, duration, and exit_code come from the per-attempt
23	// result (run record or bundle result.json). Harness/provider/model are
24	// taken from the result when present and enriched from kind:routing /
25	// kind:escalation-summary bead events otherwise.
26	type Attempt struct {
27		AttemptID             string    `json:"attempt_id"`
28		BeadID                string    `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
29		Harness               string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
30		Provider              string    `json:"provider,omitempty"`
31		Model                 string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
32		Tier                  string    `json:"tier,omitempty"`
33		Status                string    `json:"status,omitempty"`
34		Outcome               string    `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
35		Bucket                Bucket    `json:"bucket,omitempty"`
36		CostUSD               float64   `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
37		DurationMS            int       `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
38		ExitCode              int       `json:"exit_code"`
39		StartedAt             time.Time `json:"started_at,omitempty"`
40		FinishedAt            time.Time `json:"finished_at,omitempty"`
41		InputTokens           int       `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
42		OutputTokens          int       `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
43		Source                string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
44		RoutingIntentSource   string    `json:"routing_intent_source,omitempty"`
45		RequestedTier         string    `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
46		SmartJustification    string    `json:"smart_justification,omitempty"`
47		RejectedRoutePinCount int       `json:"rejected_route_pin_count,omitempty"`
48		RoutingIntentDegraded bool      `json:"routing_intent_degraded,omitempty"`
49		RoutingIntentNote     string    `json:"routing_intent_note,omitempty"`
50	}

=== DDx CODEBASE STRUCTURE ===

1. DIRECTORY LAYOUT
   cli/internal/agent/           - Execute loop, bead execution logic
   cli/internal/bead/            - Bead storage, lifecycle, types
   cli/internal/exec/            - Execution runtime (metrics, state, result storage)
   cli/internal/agentmetrics/    - Metrics aggregation for attempts
   cli/cmd/                      - CLI command definitions
   
2. KEY CONCEPTS
   - Execute loop: Drains execution-ready queue of beads
   - Bead: Work item with metadata, status, dependencies
   - Attempt: Single try to execute a bead
   - Harness: Execution environment (agent, command, etc.)
   - Model: LLM selection (paired with harness)

=== KEY FILES IDENTIFIED ===

EXECUTE LOOP:
- execute_bead_loop.go (130KB) - Main loop driver, event emission
- execute_bead.go (95KB) - Single bead execution
- types.go - Core agent types

FORMATS & SCHEMAS:
- events.jsonl format:
  * Location: .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
  * Structure: Per-line JSON objects
  * Fields: session_id, type, ts, data

- ProgressEvent (channel-based):
  * event_id, attempt_id, worker_id, project_id, bead_id
  * harness, model, profile, phase, phase_seq
  * heartbeat, ts, elapsed_ms, message

- ExecuteBeadReport (loop result item):
  * bead_id, attempt_id, worker_id, harness, provider, model
  * status, detail, error, stderr
  * actual_power, predicted_power, predicted_speed_tps
  * cost_usd, duration_ms
  * review_verdict, review_rationale
  * no_changes_rationale, tier, probe_result
  * various git refs (base_rev, result_rev, implement_rev, etc)
  * disruption tracking, resource exhaustion info

- result.json & manifest.json:
  * Located in .ddx/executions/*/
  * RunManifest: run_id, definition_id, status, exit_code, timestamps
  * RunResult: metric observation, stdout, stderr, value, unit

COMMAND LAYER (cli/cmd/):
- work.go - Main queue drain command
- agent_execute_bead.go - Single bead execution command
- agent_execute_loop.go - Full loop command
- agent_metrics.go - Metrics collection/reporting

BEAD STORAGE:
- .ddx/bead/*.json - Individual bead records
- Lifecycle states: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled
- BeadEvent: append-only records (kind, summary, body, actor, created_at)

EOF
cat /tmp/ddx_structure_summary.txt

input.command:
cat << 'EOF'
================================================================================
                       DDx CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT
================================================================================

PROJECT STRUCTURE
=================

Directory Hierarchy:
  cli/internal/agent/              - Queue drain loop, bead execution engine
  cli/internal/bead/               - Bead persistence, lifecycle, storage
  cli/internal/exec/               - Execution result formats and storage
  cli/internal/agentmetrics/       - Story 11 metrics aggregation
  cli/internal/config/             - Config resolution and schema
  cli/internal/escalation/         - Power tier escalation logic
  cli/internal/checks/             - Pre-merge/pre-land gate checks
  cli/cmd/                         - CLI command definitions
  cli/internal/server/             - Server mode (SvelteKit frontend)


================================================================================
1. EXECUTE LOOP ARCHITECTURE (cli/internal/agent/)
================================================================================

PRIMARY FILES:
  ✓ execute_bead_loop.go (130 KB)  - Main loop driver, RuntimeConfig, event emission
  ✓ execute_bead.go (95 KB)        - Single bead execution orchestrator
  ✓ types.go                        - Core agent types (Result, Config, etc.)

SUBDIRECTORIES:
  cli/internal/agent/executeloop/  - Loop mode enums and constants
  cli/internal/agent/try/          - Single attempt execution
  cli/internal/agent/work/         - Queue abstraction and picking
  cli/internal/agent/escalation/   - Tier escalation policies
  cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/  - Provider health probing

RUNTIME STRUCTURE:
  ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
    Log             io.Writer           - Event/progress logging
    EventSink       io.Writer           - JSONL event sink (writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl)
    ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent - Non-blocking channel for real-time progress
    PreClaimHook    func()              - Pre-claim validation
    PreClaimIntakeHook                  - Intake classification (actionable/scope check)
    PostAttemptTriageHook               - After attempt, before next iteration
    ReviewCostCap   *escalation.CostCapTracker - Optional review budget tracking
    RoutePreflight  func() error        - Upstream route validation
    BudgetStop      func() (report, bool) - Pre-claim budget check
    Mode            executeloop.Mode    - Loop mode (Once, Drain, Watch, etc.)
    IdleInterval    time.Duration       - Poll interval in watch mode
    SessionID       string              - Session identifier
    WorkerID        string              - Worker identifier
    ProjectRoot     string              - Project root directory
    TargetBeadID    string              - Optional single-bead target
  }


================================================================================
2. EVENTS & METRICS FORMAT
================================================================================

A. EVENTS.JSONL FORMAT
   Location: .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
   Format:   Newline-delimited JSON
   
   Per-line structure:
   {
     "session_id": "<uuid>",
     "type": "<event-type-string>",  // e.g., "loop.start", "pre_claim_intake.start", "attempt.complete"
     "ts": "2026-05-10T18:16:00.000Z", // RFC3339Nano timestamp
     "data": { /* event-specific payload */ }
   }
   
   Event types written by execute_bead_loop.go:
   - loop.start              - Loop initialization
   - loop.active             - Active processing started
   - loop.idle               - Waiting for ready candidates
   - pre_claim_intake.start  - Intake classification begins
   - attempt.start           - Bead execution attempt starts
   - attempt.complete        - Attempt finished
   - attempt.skipped         - Bead was skipped
   - attempt.landed          - Code changes landed
   - (custom)                - Decomposition, disruption, etc.

B. PROGRESS EVENT (CHANNEL-BASED)
   Sent to runtime.ProgressCh during loop execution
   Used for real-time server-side progress tracking
   
   ProgressEvent struct {
     EventID   string    // "evt-<random>"
     AttemptID string    // Unique attempt identifier
     WorkerID  string    // Worker instance ID
     ProjectID string    // Project/workspace ID
     BeadID    string    // Current bead being processed
     Harness   string    // Execution harness (agent, command, etc.)
     Model     string    // Model/version identifier
     Profile   string    // Routing profile (default, cheap, fast, smart)
     Phase     string    // Current phase (loop.active, pre_claim_intake, attempt.xxx)
     PhaseSeq  int       // Sequence number within phase
     Heartbeat bool      // True for keep-alive signals
     TS        time.Time // Timestamp
     ElapsedMS int64     // Milliseconds since loop/attempt start
     Message   string    // Optional status message
   }

C. EXECUTE BEAD REPORT (LOOP RESULT)
   ExecuteBeadReport struct {
     BeadID    string            // Bead being executed
     AttemptID string            // Unique attempt ID
     WorkerID  string
     Harness   string            // e.g., "agent", "command"
     Provider  string            // e.g., "anthropic", "openai"
     Model     string            // Model identifier
     ActualPower int             // Routing power of actual model
     PredictedPower int          // Pre-execution power prediction
     PredictedSpeedTPS float64   // Tokens/second prediction
     PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64
     PredictedCostSource string   // Where prediction came from
     
     Status    string            // success, failed, no_changes, etc.
     Detail    string            // Free-form detail
     Error     string            // Error message
     Stderr    string            // Process stderr
     
     // Git revisions
     BaseRev        string        // Starting commit
     ResultRev      string        // Final commit after landing
     ImplementationRev string     // Worker's implementation commit
     LandedRev      string        // Target branch tip after merge
     EvidenceRev    string        // Evidence commit if separate
     PreserveRef    string        // Preserved iteration ref
     CandidateRef   string        // refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>
     CycleIndex     int           // Zero-based repair cycle
     
     // Review outcomes
     ReviewVerdict   string       // APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK
     ReviewRationale string       // Reviewer feedback
     
     // Attempt details
     NoChangesRationale string    // Why no changes were made
     Tier            string       // Model tier (cheap, standard, smart)
     ProbeResult     string       // Provider health probe result
     CostUSD         float64      // Actual billed cost
     DurationMS      int64        // Wall-clock duration
     RateLimitBudget time.Duration // Rate-limit retry budget used
     
     // Disruption tracking
     Disrupted       bool         // Was attempt disrupted?
     DisruptionReason string      // Kind of disruption
     OutcomeReason   string       // Machine-readable lifecycle classification
     
     // Decomposition (when status == declined_needs_decomposition)
     DecompositionRecommendation []string // Recommended sub-bead titles
     DecompositionRationale string         // Explanation
     
     // Resource limits
     ResourceExhausted any         // Preflight exhaustion result
   }

D. EXECUTE LOOP RESULT
   ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
     Attempts          int                  // Total attempts made
     Successes         int                  // Successful attempts
     Failures          int                  // Failed attempts
     StopCondition     string               // drained|blocked|deferred|no_progress|signal
     ExitReason        string               // Reason for exit
     NoReadyWork       bool                 // No candidates available
     NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown  // Breakdown of blockers
     QueueSnapshot     *QueueSnapshot       // Queue state snapshot
     LastSuccessAt     time.Time            // Time of last success
     LastFailureStatus string               // Status of last failure
     Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  // Per-attempt reports
   }


================================================================================
3. BEAD STORAGE & LIFECYCLE (cli/internal/bead/)
================================================================================

BEAD TYPE:
   Bead struct {
     ID          string           // Unique identifier
     Title       string           // Work summary
     Status      string           // See CanonicalStatuses below
     Priority    int              // 0-4 tier ranking
     IssueType   string           // "task", "operator-prompt", etc.
     Owner       string           // Assignee
     CreatedAt   time.Time
     CreatedBy   string
     UpdatedAt   time.Time
     
     // Optional fields
     Labels      []string         // e.g., ["kind:task", "effort:large"]
     Parent      string           // Parent bead ID
     Description string           // Full text
     Acceptance  string           // Acceptance criteria
     Notes       string           // Internal notes
     Dependencies []Dependency    // Blocked-by relationships
   }
   
   Status Constants (CanonicalStatuses):
   - open               // Ready for work
   - in_progress        // Currently being worked
   - closed             // Completed
   - blocked            // Explicit external blocker
   - proposed           // Awaiting operator approval
   - cancelled          // Will not run

BEAD EVENTS (Append-only log):
   BeadEvent struct {
     Kind      string       // e.g., "routing", "escalation-summary", "disruption"
     Summary   string       // Brief title
     Body      string       // Full content
     Actor     string       // Who created it
     CreatedAt time.Time
     Source    string       // e.g., "execute-bead-loop"
   }

STORAGE PATHS:
   .ddx/bead/              - Bead records directory
   .ddx/attachments/       - Per-bead attachment directory
   .ddx/executions/        - Execution results (legacy format)


================================================================================
4. EXECUTION RESULT FORMATS (cli/internal/exec/)
================================================================================

RunManifest (result manifest):
   Located: .ddx/executions/<run-id>/manifest.json
   RunManifest struct {
     RunID            string            // Unique execution run ID
     DefinitionID     string            // Definition identifier
     ArtifactIDs      []string          // Artifact dependencies
     ProducesArtifact string            // Artifact produced
     StartedAt        time.Time
     FinishedAt       time.Time
     Status           string            // success|failed|timed_out|errored
     ExitCode         int               // Process exit code
     MergeBlocking    bool              // Blocks merge if true
     AgentSessionID   string            // Cross-reference to agent session
     Attachments      map[string]string // Artifact URL map
     Provenance       Provenance        // Actor, host, git, version
   }

RunResult (execution output):
   Located: .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json
   RunResult struct {
     Metric *MetricObservation  // Structured metric result
     Stdout string              // Command output
     Stderr string              // Error output
     Value  float64             // Numeric value
     Unit   string              // Measurement unit
     Parsed bool                // Was value parsed?
   }

MetricObservation:
   MetricObservation struct {
     ArtifactID   string           // Artifact being measured
     DefinitionID string           // Metric definition
     ObservedAt   time.Time
     Status       string           // success|failed|etc
     Value        float64          // Measured value
     Unit         string           // e.g., "ms", "bytes"
     Samples      []float64        // Multiple samples
     Comparison   ComparisonResult // Baseline comparison
   }


================================================================================
5. COMMAND LAYER (cli/cmd/)
================================================================================

PRIMARY COMMANDS:
  work.go              - "ddx work" queue drain (FEAT-010 layer 3)
  agent_execute_bead.go - "ddx try" single bead execution (layer 2)
  agent_execute_loop.go - "ddx agent execute-loop" full loop
  agent_metrics.go     - "ddx metrics" aggregation and reporting

WORK COMMAND FLAGS:
  --project <path>               - Target project root (default: CWD git root)
  --from <rev>                   - Base revision (default: HEAD)
  --harness <name>               - Execution harness (passthrough)
  --model <id>                   - Model constraint (passthrough)
  --profile <name>               - Routing profile (default|cheap|fast|smart)
  --provider <name>              - Provider constraint (passthrough)
  --once                         - Execute at most one bead
  --watch                        - Keep watching for new work
  --idle-interval <duration>     - Poll interval (default: 30s)
  --no-review                    - Skip post-merge review
  --min-power <int>              - Minimum model power
  --max-power <int>              - Maximum model power
  --max-cost <usd>               - Stop on budget exhausted
  --max-bead-cost <usd>          - Per-bead cost limit
  --json                         - Output as JSON

COMMAND STRUCTURE:
  runWork(cmd, args)
    ├─ checkProjectRuntimePreflight()
    ├─ resolveProjectRoot()
    └─ runAgentExecuteLoopImpl()
        ├─ resolveResolvedConfig()      - Build durable config knobs
        ├─ ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()      - Main loop
        └─ outputResult()               - Format/display results


================================================================================
6. KEY PACKAGES
================================================================================

cli/internal/config/         - Configuration resolution (ResolvedConfig)
cli/internal/escalation/     - Tier escalation and cost tracking
cli/internal/evidence/       - Evidence attachment and archival
cli/internal/checks/         - Pre-merge and pre-land gate validation
cli/internal/metric/         - Metrics definitions and tracking
cli/internal/agentmetrics/   - Per-attempt metrics aggregation (Story 11)
cli/internal/processmetrics/ - Process-level metrics collection
cli/internal/docgraph/       - Document relationship graph
cli/internal/registry/       - Artifact and definition registry
cli/internal/server/         - Web UI server (SvelteKit frontend)


================================================================================
7. METRICS AGGREGATION (Story 11 - cli/internal/agentmetrics/)
================================================================================

Attempt struct (agentmetrics package):
   AttemptID          string
   BeadID             string
   Harness            string
   Provider           string
   Model              string
   Tier               string
   Status             string      // success|failed|no_changes|etc
   Outcome            string
   CostUSD            float64
   DurationMS         int
   ExitCode           int
   StartedAt          time.Time
   FinishedAt         time.Time
   InputTokens        int
   OutputTokens       int
   Source             string      // "run-store" or "bundle"
   RoutingIntentSource string      // Where routing decision came from
   RequestedTier      string
   SmartJustification string
   RejectedRoutePinCount int
   RoutingIntentDegraded bool

Data Sources (priority order):
  1. .ddx/exec/runs/*.json       - Modern run-store format
  2. .ddx/executions/*/result.json - Legacy bundle format
  3. Bead events (kind:routing, kind:escalation-summary) - Enrichment

Deduplication:
  Per-attempt records are deduped by AttemptID — run-store wins over bundles


================================================================================
8. METRICS & MEASUREMENTS
================================================================================

Cost Tracking:
  - escalation.CostCapTracker    - Accumulates cost across attempts
  - float64 CostUSD              - Per-attempt cost
  - Per-bead label: budget:<USD> - Per-bead cost limit override
  - CLI flag: --max-bead-cost    - Default per-bead limit
  - CLI flag: --max-cost         - Global cost cap

Duration Tracking:
  - int64 DurationMS             - Attempt wall-clock time
  - ExecuteBeadLoopResult.LastSuccessAt
  - Timestamps in events.jsonl

Token Metrics:
  - InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedTokens, TotalTokens
  - Populated by harness and recorded in Result struct

Power/Tier Metrics:
  - ActualPower                  - Runtime model power level
  - PredictedPower               - Pre-execution prediction
  - Tier                         - Model tier (cheap|standard|smart)
  - EscalationCount              - Tier escalations performed


================================================================================
9. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE SUMMARY
================================================================================

.ddx/
├─ bead/                    # Bead records (JSONL + metadata)
├─ attachments/             # Per-bead evidence and events
│  └─ <bead-id>/
│     └─ events.jsonl       # Bead execution timeline
├─ executions/              # Execution results (legacy)
│  └─ <run-id>/
│     ├─ manifest.json
│     └─ result.json
├─ exec/
│  └─ runs/                 # Modern run-store (per-attempt records)
│     └─ <run-id>.json
├─ sessions/                # Agent session logs
├─ artifacts/               # Generated artifacts
└─ config/                  # Project config

Git structures:
└─ refs/ddx/iterations/     # Candidate branch refs
   └─ <attempt-id>/<cycle-index>


================================================================================
10. INTEGRATION POINTS
================================================================================

Event Flow:
  1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.EventSink receives writeLoopEvent() calls
  2. Events written to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
  3. ProgressEvent channel sends to server/UI for real-time updates
  4. ExecuteBeadReport collected in ExecuteBeadLoopResult

Result Flow:
  1. ExecuteBeadReport collected per attempt
  2. Aggregated in ExecuteBeadLoopResult.Results
  3. Stored in .ddx/exec/runs/<run-id>.json (run-store)
  4. Consumed by agentmetrics.Loader for Story 11 aggregation

Configuration Flow:
  1. CLI flags parsed → RouteFlags
  2. ResolvedConfig built by resolveResolvedConfig()
  3. Passed to ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() as rcfg
  4. Agent service uses rcfg for routing decisions

================================================================================
EOF

data.output:
================================================================================
                       DDx CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT
================================================================================

PROJECT STRUCTURE
=================

Directory Hierarchy:
  cli/internal/agent/              - Queue drain loop, bead execution engine
  cli/internal/bead/               - Bead persistence, lifecycle, storage
  cli/internal/exec/               - Execution result formats and storage
  cli/internal/agentmetrics/       - Story 11 metrics aggregation
  cli/internal/config/             - Config resolution and schema
  cli/internal/escalation/         - Power tier escalation logic
  cli/internal/checks/             - Pre-merge/pre-land gate checks
  cli/cmd/                         - CLI command definitions
  cli/internal/server/             - Server mode (SvelteKit frontend)


================================================================================
1. EXECUTE LOOP ARCHITECTURE (cli/internal/agent/)
================================================================================

PRIMARY FILES:
  ✓ execute_bead_loop.go (130 KB)  - Main loop driver, RuntimeConfig, event emission
  ✓ execute_bead.go (95 KB)        - Single bead execution orchestrator
  ✓ types.go                        - Core agent types (Result, Config, etc.)

SUBDIRECTORIES:
  cli/internal/agent/executeloop/  - Loop mode enums and constants
  cli/internal/agent/try/          - Single attempt execution
  cli/internal/agent/work/         - Queue abstraction and picking
  cli/internal/agent/escalation/   - Tier escalation policies
  cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/  - Provider health probing

RUNTIME STRUCTURE:
  ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
    Log             io.Writer           - Event/progress logging
    EventSink       io.Writer           - JSONL event sink (writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl)
    ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent - Non-blocking channel for real-time progress
    PreClaimHook    func()              - Pre-claim validation
    PreClaimIntakeHook                  - Intake classification (actionable/scope check)
    PostAttemptTriageHook               - After attempt, before next iteration
    ReviewCostCap   *escalation.CostCapTracker - Optional review budget tracking
    RoutePreflight  func() error        - Upstream route validation
    BudgetStop      func() (report, bool) - Pre-claim budget check
    Mode            executeloop.Mode    - Loop mode (Once, Drain, Watch, etc.)
    IdleInterval    time.Duration       - Poll interval in watch mode
    SessionID       string              - Session identifier
    WorkerID        string              - Worker identifier
    ProjectRoot     string              - Project root directory
    TargetBeadID    string              - Optional single-bead target
  }


================================================================================
2. EVENTS & METRICS FORMAT
================================================================================

A. EVENTS.JSONL FORMAT
   Location: .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
   Format:   Newline-delimited JSON
   
   Per-line structure:
   {
     "session_id": "<uuid>",
     "type": "<event-type-string>",  // e.g., "loop.start", "pre_claim_intake.start", "attempt.complete"
     "ts": "2026-05-10T18:16:00.000Z", // RFC3339Nano timestamp
     "data": { /* event-specific payload */ }
   }
   
   Event types written by execute_bead_loop.go:
   - loop.start              - Loop initialization
   - loop.active             - Active processing started
   - loop.idle               - Waiting for ready candidates
   - pre_claim_intake.start  - Intake classification begins
   - attempt.start           - Bead execution attempt starts
   - attempt.complete        - Attempt finished
   - attempt.skipped         - Bead was skipped
   - attempt.landed          - Code changes landed
   - (custom)                - Decomposition, disruption, etc.

B. PROGRESS EVENT (CHANNEL-BASED)
   Sent to runtime.ProgressCh during loop execution
   Used for real-time server-side progress tracking
   
   ProgressEvent struct {
     EventID   string    // "evt-<random>"
     AttemptID string    // Unique attempt identifier
     WorkerID  string    // Worker instance ID
     ProjectID string    // Project/workspace ID
     BeadID    string    // Current bead being processed
     Harness   string    // Execution harness (agent, command, etc.)
     Model     string    // Model/version identifier
     Profile   string    // Routing profile (default, cheap, fast, smart)
     Phase     string    // Current phase (loop.active, pre_claim_intake, attempt.xxx)
     PhaseSeq  int       // Sequence number within phase
     Heartbeat bool      // True for keep-alive signals
     TS        time.Time // Timestamp
     ElapsedMS int64     // Milliseconds since loop/attempt start
     Message   string    // Optional status message
   }

C. EXECUTE BEAD REPORT (LOOP RESULT)
   ExecuteBeadReport struct {
     BeadID    string            // Bead being executed
     AttemptID string            // Unique attempt ID
     WorkerID  string
     Harness   string            // e.g., "agent", "command"
     Provider  string            // e.g., "anthropic", "openai"
     Model     string            // Model identifier
     ActualPower int             // Routing power of actual model
     PredictedPower int          // Pre-execution power prediction
     PredictedSpeedTPS float64   // Tokens/second prediction
     PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64
     PredictedCostSource string   // Where prediction came from
     
     Status    string            // success, failed, no_changes, etc.
     Detail    string            // Free-form detail
     Error     string            // Error message
     Stderr    string            // Process stderr
     
     // Git revisions
     BaseRev        string        // Starting commit
     ResultRev      string        // Final commit after landing
     ImplementationRev string     // Worker's implementation commit
     LandedRev      string        // Target branch tip after merge
     EvidenceRev    string        // Evidence commit if separate
     PreserveRef    string        // Preserved iteration ref
     CandidateRef   string        // refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>
     CycleIndex     int           // Zero-based repair cycle
     
     // Review outcomes
     ReviewVerdict   string       // APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK
     ReviewRationale string       // Reviewer feedback
     
     // Attempt details
     NoChangesRationale string    // Why no changes were made
     Tier            string       // Model tier (cheap, standard, smart)
     ProbeResult     string       // Provider health probe result
     CostUSD         float64      // Actual billed cost
     DurationMS      int64        // Wall-clock duration
     RateLimitBudget time.Duration // Rate-limit retry budget used
     
     // Disruption tracking
     Disrupted       bool         // Was attempt disrupted?
     DisruptionReason string      // Kind of disruption
     OutcomeReason   string       // Machine-readable lifecycle classification
     
     // Decomposition (when status == declined_needs_decomposition)
     DecompositionRecommendation []string // Recommended sub-bead titles
     DecompositionRationale string         // Explanation
     
     // Resource limits
     ResourceExhausted any         // Preflight exhaustion result
   }

D. EXECUTE LOOP RESULT
   ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
     Attempts          int                  // Total attempts made
     Successes         int                  // Successful attempts
     Failures          int                  // Failed attempts
     StopCondition     string               // drained|blocked|deferred|no_progress|signal
     ExitReason        string               // Reason for exit
     NoReadyWork       bool                 // No candidates available
     NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown  // Breakdown of blockers
     QueueSnapshot     *QueueSnapshot       // Queue state snapshot
     LastSuccessAt     time.Time            // Time of last success
     LastFailureStatus string               // Status of last failure
     Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  // Per-attempt reports
   }


================================================================================
3. BEAD STORAGE & LIFECYCLE (cli/internal/bead/)
================================================================================

BEAD TYPE:
   Bead struct {
     ID          string           // Unique identifier
     Title       string           // Work summary
     Status      string           // See CanonicalStatuses below
     Priority    int              // 0-4 tier ranking
     IssueType   string           // "task", "operator-prompt", etc.
     Owner       string           // Assignee
     CreatedAt   time.Time
     CreatedBy   string
     UpdatedAt   time.Time
     
     // Optional fields
     Labels      []string         // e.g., ["kind:task", "effort:large"]
     Parent      string           // Parent bead ID
     Description string           // Full text
     Acceptance  string           // Acceptance criteria
     Notes       string           // Internal notes
     Dependencies []Dependency    // Blocked-by relationships
   }
   
   Status Constants (CanonicalStatuses):
   - open               // Ready for work
   - in_progress        // Currently being worked
   - closed             // Completed
   - blocked            // Explicit external blocker
   - proposed           // Awaiting operator approval
   - cancelled          // Will not run

BEAD EVENTS (Append-only log):
   BeadEvent struct {
     Kind      string       // e.g., "routing", "escalation-summary", "disruption"
     Summary   string       // Brief title
     Body      string       // Full content
     Actor     string       // Who created it
     CreatedAt time.Time
     Source    string       // e.g., "execute-bead-loop"
   }

STORAGE PATHS:
   .ddx/bead/              - Bead records directory
   .ddx/attachments/       - Per-bead attachment directory
   .ddx/executions/        - Execution results (legacy format)


================================================================================
4. EXECUTION RESULT FORMATS (cli/internal/exec/)
================================================================================

RunManifest (result manifest):
   Located: .ddx/executions/<run-id>/manifest.json
   RunManifest struct {
     RunID            string            // Unique execution run ID
     DefinitionID     string            // Definition identifier
     ArtifactIDs      []string          // Artifact dependencies
     ProducesArtifact string            // Artifact produced
     StartedAt        time.Time
     FinishedAt       time.Time
     Status           string            // success|failed|timed_out|errored
     ExitCode         int               // Process exit code
     MergeBlocking    bool              // Blocks merge if true
     AgentSessionID   string            // Cross-reference to agent session
     Attachments      map[string]string // Artifact URL map
     Provenance       Provenance        // Actor, host, git, version
   }

RunResult (execution output):
   Located: .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json
   RunResult struct {
     Metric *MetricObservation  // Structured metric result
     Stdout string              // Command output
     Stderr string              // Error output
     Value  float64             // Numeric value
     Unit   string              // Measurement unit
     Parsed bool                // Was value parsed?
   }

MetricObservation:
   MetricObservation struct {
     ArtifactID   string           // Artifact being measured
     DefinitionID string           // Metric definition
     ObservedAt   time.Time
     Status       string           // success|failed|etc
     Value        float64          // Measured value
     Unit         string           // e.g., "ms", "bytes"
     Samples      []float64        // Multiple samples
     Comparison   ComparisonResult // Baseline comparison
   }


================================================================================
5. COMMAND LAYER (cli/cmd/)
================================================================================

PRIMARY COMMANDS:
  work.go              - "ddx work" queue drain (FEAT-010 layer 3)
  agent_execute_bead.go - "ddx try" single bead execution (layer 2)
  agent_execute_loop.go - "ddx agent execute-loop" full loop
  agent_metrics.go     - "ddx metrics" aggregation and reporting

WORK COMMAND FLAGS:
  --project <path>               - Target project root (default: CWD git root)
  --from <rev>                   - Base revision (default: HEAD)
  --harness <name>               - Execution harness (passthrough)
  --model <id>                   - Model constraint (passthrough)
  --profile <name>               - Routing profile (default|cheap|fast|smart)
  --provider <name>              - Provider constraint (passthrough)
  --once                         - Execute at most one bead
  --watch                        - Keep watching for new work
  --idle-interval <duration>     - Poll interval (default: 30s)
  --no-review                    - Skip post-merge review
  --min-power <int>              - Minimum model power
  --max-power <int>              - Maximum model power
  --max-cost <usd>               - Stop on budget exhausted
  --max-bead-cost <usd>          - Per-bead cost limit
  --json                         - Output as JSON

COMMAND STRUCTURE:
  runWork(cmd, args)
    ├─ checkProjectRuntimePreflight()
    ├─ resolveProjectRoot()
    └─ runAgentExecuteLoopImpl()
        ├─ resolveResolvedConfig()      - Build durable config knobs
        ├─ ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()      - Main loop
        └─ outputResult()               - Format/display results


================================================================================
6. KEY PACKAGES
================================================================================

cli/internal/config/         - Configuration resolution (ResolvedConfig)
cli/internal/escalation/     - Tier escalation and cost tracking
cli/internal/evidence/       - Evidence attachment and archival
cli/internal/checks/         - Pre-merge and pre-land gate validation
cli/internal/metric/         - Metrics definitions and tracking
cli/internal/agentmetrics/   - Per-attempt metrics aggregation (Story 11)
cli/internal/processmetrics/ - Process-level metrics collection
cli/internal/docgraph/       - Document relationship graph
cli/internal/registry/       - Artifact and definition registry
cli/internal/server/         - Web UI server (SvelteKit frontend)


================================================================================
7. METRICS AGGREGATION (Story 11 - cli/internal/agentmetrics/)
================================================================================

Attempt struct (agentmetrics package):
   AttemptID          string
   BeadID             string
   Harness            string
   Provider           string
   Model              string
   Tier               string
   Status             string      // success|failed|no_changes|etc
   Outcome            string
   CostUSD            float64
   DurationMS         int
   ExitCode           int
   StartedAt          time.Time
   FinishedAt         time.Time
   InputTokens        int
   OutputTokens       int
   Source             string      // "run-store" or "bundle"
   RoutingIntentSource string      // Where routing decision came from
   RequestedTier      string
   SmartJustification string
   RejectedRoutePinCount int
   RoutingIntentDegraded bool

Data Sources (priority order):
  1. .ddx/exec/runs/*.json       - Modern run-store format
  2. .ddx/executions/*/result.json - Legacy bundle format
  3. Bead events (kind:routing, kind:escalation-summary) - Enrichment

Deduplication:
  Per-attempt records are deduped by AttemptID — run-store wins over bundles


================================================================================
8. METRICS & MEASUREMENTS
================================================================================

Cost Tracking:
  - escalation.CostCapTracker    - Accumulates cost across attempts
  - float64 CostUSD              - Per-attempt cost
  - Per-bead label: budget:<USD> - Per-bead cost limit override
  - CLI flag: --max-bead-cost    - Default per-bead limit
  - CLI flag: --max-cost         - Global cost cap

Duration Tracking:
  - int64 DurationMS             - Attempt wall-clock time
  - ExecuteBeadLoopResult.LastSuccessAt
  - Timestamps in events.jsonl

Token Metrics:
  - InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedTokens, TotalTokens
  - Populated by harness and recorded in Result struct

Power/Tier Metrics:
  - ActualPower                  - Runtime model power level
  - PredictedPower               - Pre-execution prediction
  - Tier                         - Model tier (cheap|standard|smart)
  - EscalationCount              - Tier escalations performed


================================================================================
9. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE SUMMARY
================================================================================

.ddx/
├─ bead/                    # Bead records (JSONL + metadata)
├─ attachments/             # Per-bead evidence and events
│  └─ <bead-id>/
│     └─ events.jsonl       # Bead execution timeline
├─ executions/              # Execution results (legacy)
│  └─ <run-id>/
│     ├─ manifest.json
│     └─ result.json
├─ exec/
│  └─ runs/                 # Modern run-store (per-attempt records)
│     └─ <run-id>.json
├─ sessions/                # Agent session logs
├─ artifacts/               # Generated artifacts
└─ config/                  # Project config

Git structures:
└─ refs/ddx/iterations/     # Candidate branch refs
   └─ <attempt-id>/<cycle-index>


================================================================================
10. INTEGRATION POINTS
================================================================================

Event Flow:
  1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.EventSink receives writeLoopEvent() calls
  2. Events written to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
  3. ProgressEvent channel sends to server/UI for real-time updates
  4. ExecuteBeadReport collected in ExecuteBeadLoopResult

Result Flow:
  1. ExecuteBeadReport collected per attempt
  2. Aggregated in ExecuteBeadLoopResult.Results
  3. Stored in .ddx/exec/runs/<run-id>.json (run-store)
  4. Consumed by agentmetrics.Loader for Story 11 aggregation

Configuration Flow:
  1. CLI flags parsed → RouteFlags
  2. ResolvedConfig built by resolveResolvedConfig()
  3. Passed to ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() as rcfg
  4. Agent service uses rcfg for routing decisions

================================================================================

================================================================================
CRITICAL STRUCTURES REFERENCE
================================================================================

ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime:

EVENT TYPES WRITTEN TO events.jsonl
================================================================================

Loop lifecycle:

  - loop.idle
  - loop.stop

Attempt lifecycle:

COMMAND EXECUTION CHAIN
================================================================================

cli/cmd/work.go:runWork()

    ├─ resolveResolvedConfig() [config.ResolvedConfig created]

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()

  └─ return ExecuteBeadLoopResult{Results: reports, ...}
  ↓
Output:

STORED FILE LOCATIONS & FORMATS

   Stored in: Bead.Extra["events"] or separate attachment
   Type:      []BeadEvent
   Example:   {kind: "routing", summary: "...", body: "...", actor: "...", ...}

================================================================================

Perfect! Now let me create a final comprehensive summary document:

## Summary

I've completed a thorough exploration of the DDx codebase focusing on the execute loop, events, and metrics infrastructure. Here's what I found:

### Key Files and Locations

**Core Execute Loop** (`cli/internal/agent/`):
- `execute_bead_loop.go` (130 KB) - Main loop driver with `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, `ProgressEvent`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, and event emission logic
- `execute_bead.go` (95 KB) - Single bead execution orchestrator
- `types.go` - Core agent types including `Result` and `Config`

**Event & Metrics** (`cli/internal/agent/` and `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`):
- `execute_bead_loop.go:writeLoopEvent()` (lines 2350-2369) - Writes JSONL events
- `agentmetrics/types.go` - `Attempt` struct for Story 11 aggregation
- `agentmetrics/loader.go` - Metrics loading and aggregation

### Events Format

**Location**: `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl`

**Structure**: Newline-delimited JSON with fields:
- `session_id` - Session identifier
- `type` - Event type (loop.start, attempt.complete, etc.)
- `ts` - RFC3339Nano timestamp
- `data` - Event-specific payload

**writeLoopEvent() Function** emits:
```go
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "type": "event_type",
  "ts": "2026-05-10T18:16:00.000Z",
  "data": { /* event payload */ }
}
```

### ProgressEvent Structure

Sent via non-blocking channel to real-time server updates:
- `event_id`, `attempt_id`, `worker_id`, `project_id`, `bead_id`
- `harness`, `model`, `profile`, `phase`, `phase_seq`
- `heartbeat`, `ts`, `elapsed_ms`, `message`

### ExecuteBeadReport Structure

Per-attempt result record containing:
- Status, detail, error, stderr
- Hardware metrics: `actual_power`, `predicted_power`, `cost_usd`, `duration_ms`
- Git refs: `base_rev`, `result_rev`, `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`
- Review outcomes: `review_verdict`, `review_rationale`
- Attempt details: `no_changes_rationale`, `tier`, `probe_result`
- Disruption tracking: `disrupted`, `disruption_reason`, `outcome_reason`

### Result.json & Manifest.json

**Modern Format** (`.ddx/exec/runs/<run-id>.json`):
- Direct serialization of `ExecuteBeadReport`

**Legacy Format** (`.ddx/executions/<run-id>/`):
- `manifest.json` - `RunManifest` struct (run_id, status, timestamps, exit_code, agent_session_id)
- `result.json` - `RunResult` struct (stdout, stderr, value, unit, metric observations)

### Bead Storage

- Location: `.ddx/bead/<bead-id>.json`
- Lifecycle states: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled
- Append-only events: `BeadEvent` with kind, summary, body, actor, timestamp

### Command Execution Chain

`ddx work` → `cli/cmd/work.go:runWork()` → `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` initialized → `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()` → Per-attempt `ExecuteBeadReport` collected in `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` → Output as JSON/events/progress channel

### Metrics Aggregation (Story 11)

`agentmetrics.Attempt` struct loaded from:
1. `.ddx/exec/runs/*.json` (modern run-store, priority 1)
2. `.ddx/executions/*/result.json` (legacy bundle, priority 2)
3. Bead events for enrichment (routing, escalation-summary)

All absolute file paths are under `/cli/internal/agent/` and related subdirectories as documented in the comprehensive report above.

1	// Package agentmetrics loads attempt outcomes for Story 11 aggregations
2	// (success rate, cost, duration per harness/provider/model/tier).
3	//
4	// Source priority is FEAT-010 run-store first (.ddx/exec/runs/*.json) with
5	// .ddx/executions/*/result.json as the legacy fallback. Per-attempt records
6	// are deduped by AttemptID — run-store wins over bundles when both exist —
7	// matching the multi-source dedupe used by state_runs.go (ddx-65543530).
8	//
9	// Per the Story 11 locked decision, the already_satisfied terminal status
10	// counts as success. See Bucket.Successful.
11	package agentmetrics
12	
13	import "time"
14	
15	// Source identifies where an Attempt was loaded from.
16	const (
17		SourceRunStore = "run-store"
18		SourceBundle   = "bundle"

20	
21	// Attempt is one execute-bead try projected to the fields Story 11
22	// aggregations need. Cost, duration, and exit_code come from the per-attempt
23	// result (run record or bundle result.json). Harness/provider/model are
24	// taken from the result when present and enriched from kind:routing /
25	// kind:escalation-summary bead events otherwise.

291	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
292		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook

300		if err != nil {

317		}

11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}

59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
74	)
